summary:
There is insufficient evidence to conclude that treatment of amblyopia normalises subfoveal choroidal thickness; current studies show associations but not reversal of choroidal thinning via standard therapies. Thus, the effect remains uncertain despite known alterations in choroid structure due to amblyopia.  

The summary above contains only two sentences as required. It focuses solely on clinically significant findings related directly to the query about treatment outcomes for subfoveal choroidal thickness. All other details are omitted per instructions.
